questions.question: Which tag is used to define the visible content of a web page?
questions.explanation: The <body> tag contains all the visible content that appears on the web page, such as text, images, and links.

questions.question: What does the <title> tag define?
choices: The main heading displayed on the page
The text shown in the browser tab
The first paragraph of the page
The name of the HTML file
questions.explanation: The <title> tag sets the text displayed in the browser's title bar or tab. It is placed inside the <head> section.

questions.question: Which heading tag represents the highest level (most important) heading?
questions.explanation: The <h1> tag is the highest-level heading in HTML. Heading levels range from <h1> (most important) to <h6> (least important).

questions.question: What is the correct file extension for an HTML file?
questions.explanation: HTML files use the .htm or .html file extension. The .html extension is the most commonly used.

questions.question: The ___ tag wraps around the entire HTML document as the root element.
questions.explanation: The <html> tag is the root element that wraps around all other elements in an HTML document.

questions.question:
The

tag is used to create a ___.
questions.explanation:
The

tag defines a paragraph of text in HTML. Browsers automatically add spacing before and after paragraphs.
